text.title: AI IaC Hallucination Detector Output Contract
text.summary: Pins the hallucination-report shape, finding kinds, and deprecation-handling rules.

section.title: Required hallucination-report fields
section.reference: Authoritative JSON Schema for the hallucination report.

list.item: hallucination_argument: argument name is NOT in schema for the resource type
hallucination_resource: resource type does NOT exist in schema (e.g. `aws_route53_zone_record`)
deprecation_argument: argument IS in schema but marked deprecated; carries `replacement` field
type_mismatch: argument exists but the value's type does not match schema (list vs object, string vs number)
required_missing: schema marks an argument required; diff omits it
Deprecation findings emit `replacement` field copied verbatim from the schema's deprecation_message.
Severity defaults to `advise` (vs `block` for hallucination_argument / hallucination_resource).
When the deprecation horizon is within 90 days (per provider release notes), severity upgrades to `block`.

antipattern.description: verdict `clean` while findings array is non-empty.
Finding with `recommendation` referencing a resource type not in schema.
antipattern.reason: r5 violation. Recommendations must be schema-validated.
Stale schema; deprecation horizon may have shifted.
r2 violation. Validation against unpinned schema is meaningless.

list.item: provider_versions covers every provider used in the diff.
Every finding includes a recommendation; recommendations re-validated.
verdict matches the findings array semantics (clean iff empty).
Deprecation findings carry `replacement` from schema, not LLM-generated alternative.
